weeks.title: Foundation & Core Concepts
weeks.focus: Build foundational understanding of GenAI on AWS
goals: Learn Amazon Bedrock architecture and capabilities

tasks.details: Read concept articles on FM architecture, model families, and selection criteria. Focus on understanding when to use different models.
Learn data preparation concepts and RAG architecture. Understand vector embeddings and Knowledge Bases.
Master prompt techniques: few-shot, chain-of-thought, system prompts. Try the Prompt Lab if AWS credentials are configured.
Complete Domain 1 practice questions. Review any weak areas identified.

weeks.title: Implementation & Integration
weeks.focus: Learn how to build and deploy GenAI applications
goals: Understand Bedrock Agents and agentic patterns
Learn deployment options and integration patterns

tasks.details: Learn Bedrock Agents, Action Groups, and orchestration patterns. Understand ReAct and tool calling.
Compare Bedrock vs SageMaker vs Lambda deployments. Understand when to use each option.
Learn API Gateway integration, VPC endpoints, sync vs async patterns, streaming responses.
Explore Amazon Q Business and Q Developer use cases. Understand when to use pre-built vs custom solutions.
Complete Domain 2 practice questions. Hands-on with labs if available.

weeks.title: Security, Safety & Optimization
weeks.focus: Master safety controls, security, and performance optimization

tasks.details: Learn Guardrails configuration, prompt injection defense, PII protection, and VPC endpoints.
Understand Model Cards, data lineage, compliance frameworks, and fairness evaluation.
Learn token optimization, model cascading, caching strategies, latency optimization, and streaming.
Master CloudWatch metrics, X-Ray tracing, and GenAI-specific monitoring.
Complete Domain 3 and 4 practice questions. Focus on service selection scenarios.
Learn Model Evaluations, LLM-as-a-Judge, golden datasets, and debugging techniques.
Take a timed practice exam covering all domains. Target 75+ minutes, 65 questions.
Review practice exam results. Focus study on weak areas identified.
Review Tier 1 services reference. Ensure you know key features and exam keywords for each.
Light review of exam tips and strategies. Get good rest before exam day.

tips: Focus on Domains 1 and 2 first - they comprise 57% of the exam
Understand the 'why' behind service choices, not just the 'what'
Practice recognizing exam keywords that map to specific services
Time management: ~1 minute per question, flag and move on if stuck
Read questions carefully - look for qualifiers like 'MOST', 'LEAST', 'FIRST'
